final_prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: TaraHome blog cover, landscape 3:2, clean editorial smart-home visual
Primary request: Create a warm residential smart-home cover image about choosing the right Zigbee channel for Home Assistant without showing any words.
Scene/backdrop: A bright but cozy modern living-room sideboard in soft natural window light, warm wood and off-white walls, dark-green local-control accent details.
Subject: A compact local smart-home hub and a generic Zigbee USB radio/coordinator on a short cable, placed away from a small Wi-Fi router in the background; nearby are a realistic smart bulb, contact sensor, and smart plug arranged as practical hardware. The scene should visually imply careful radio placement and a reliable local mesh, without drawn diagrams.
Composition: Premium editorial product photography, useful at small blog-card size, hardware large enough to inspect, slight depth of field, no people, no hands.
Style: realistic, polished, TaraHome direction: warm residential, dark green accents, local-first, clean and practical.
Strict avoid list: no embedded headline, no title poster, no letters, no numbers, no readable text, no logos, no brand marks, no screenshots, no UI cards, no translucent panels, no inset frames, no chart, no graph, no decorative routing lines, no network/path overlays, no glowing connection trails, no progress bars, no abstract AI diagram, no dark sci-fi scene, no messy cables.
negative_requirements: no embedded headline or title poster
no readable text, logos, or brand marks
no decorative routing, network, or path overlays
no translucent panels, inset frames, floating UI cards, charts, or progress bars
title: What Zigbee Channel Should You Use for Home Assistant?
